title: Speculum Vitae
summary: The publication artifact is the repaired-header XML at CME/source/recent_changes/additions_2023/CME90022.xml. Its medieval text descends through the University of Michigan/Middle English Compendium's 2023 structured representation from Oxford Text Archive deposit 0022, Christine Robinson's 1983 machine-readable transcription of British Library Additional MS 33995, ff. 1ra-96rb. Robinson's later 1987 doctoral thesis prints and studies the same full transcription but cannot be the source of the earlier OTA deposit. Ralph Hanna's 2008 reading edition was consulted by the 2023 encoders for lineation and divisions rather than used as the base text. No complete public manuscript facsimile was located; the British Library offers manuscript photography by request.
review_notes: The approved recent_changes XML and the legacy CME_from_OTA XML have semantically identical recovered EEBO text subtrees; their material divergence is the wrapper and repaired HEADER. Both fixities are recorded, but only the approved repaired-header file is modeled as the repository artifact.

The 1983 OTA deposit and the 1987 thesis are kept chronologically distinct. The thesis is a later scholarly realization of Robinson's transcription project, not an intermediary between the witness and OTA.
Rights and access statements are scoped to named components. Public access to a transcription or thesis does not imply permission to redistribute future British Library manuscript photographs.
The absence of a public facsimile is a dated search result as of 2026-07-14, not proof that no unindexed or private surrogate exists.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME90022
entities.description: The repaired-header XML selected for this repository's conversion and publication workflow from the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME submodule.
notes: IDNO, IDG/@ID, and VID are CME90022; BIBNO is OTA0022.

The alternate legacy file CME/source/CME_from_OTA/CME90022.xml has git blob 17b68a92ee942686afa5969d20a0b10ae47e6890 and SHA-256 15f21b8b0f525a1d5de469df8b286019362f51cfc02310351005173c669a0b0f. Its malformed wrapper uses TEMPHEAD and does not supersede the approved repaired-header artifact.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME90022
entities.description: The 2023 Middle English Compendium representation of Robinson's OTA transcription, with folio references, line numbers, hierarchical divisions, and selected manuscript headings.
notes: The header says the Middle English Compendium added markup, folio references, line numbers, and chapter divisions based on Ralph Hanna's reading edition.
Headings were drawn from the manuscript when convenient; the encoding does not attempt to distinguish y/Y from thorn and supplies no punctuation.

entities.label: Oxford Text Archive resource 0022
entities.description: Christine Robinson's plain-text machine-readable transcription of Speculum Vitae from British Library Additional MS 33995, deposited with the Oxford Text Archive on 13 May 1983.
notes: The complete transcription runs from line 1 at fol. 1ra through line 16096 at fol. 96rb.
The legacy purl.ox.ac.uk identifier is retained as historical identification only; it did not provide a working access route when checked on 2026-07-14.
The OTA item's inherited 1400-1499 date describes a broad witness-era catalog field and is not used as the work's composition date.

entities.label: Christine Robinson's machine-readable edition and doctoral thesis
entities.description: A two-volume thesis containing an introduction, a complete printed edition of Speculum Vitae from Additional MS 33995, a glossary, and authorship studies.

date_statements.label: repository date issued
date_statements.notes: The repository date is preserved without replacing the 1987 date printed on the thesis title page.

bibliographic.citation: Christine Mary Robinson, ‘A machine-readable edition of the text of the Speculum Vitae as attested in British Library MS. Additional 33995, with introduction, glossary and an investigation of claims for the common authorship of the Speculum Vitae and the Prick of Conscience’ (PhD thesis, University of Edinburgh, 1987; ERA date issued 1988).
bibliographic.title: A machine-readable edition of the text of the Speculum Vitae as attested in British Library MS. Additional 33995, with introduction, glossary and an investigation of claims for the common authorship of the Speculum Vitae and the Prick of Conscience

notes: Volume 1 contains the manuscript description, editorial preface, and full 16,096-line edition; volume 2 contains the glossary and comparative authorship studies.
The thesis reports no persuasive evidence for common authorship with the Prick of Conscience.

entities.label: Ralph Hanna's Speculum Vitae: A Reading Edition
entities.description: A two-volume Early English Text Society reading edition consulted by the 2023 Middle English Compendium encoders for line numbering and structural divisions.

bibliographic.citation: Ralph Hanna, ed., Speculum Vitae: A Reading Edition, 2 vols., Early English Text Society, Original Series 331-332 (Oxford: Oxford University Press, 2008).
bibliographic.title: Speculum Vitae: A Reading Edition
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 331-332

notes: The cached four-page ETH PDF contains only title pages and tables of contents, not the edition text or manuscript frontispieces.
Both volume title pages state that Ralph Hanna edited the work using materials assembled by Venetia Somerset.
Hanna's edition is a supporting source for the CME hierarchy, not the textual source of Robinson's 1983 transcription.

entities.label: Glover's 2024 study of The Form of Living and pastoral commonplace culture
entities.description: A peer-reviewed scholarly study used to reassess an asserted Rolle-dependent terminus post quem for Speculum Vitae; it is supporting scholarship, not a textual source or edition of Speculum Vitae.

bibliographic.citation: Timothy Glover, ‘Late-Medieval Commonplace Culture, the Pastoral Compendium, and the Form of Richard Rolle’s The Form of Living,’ Review of English Studies 75, no. 319 (2024): 165–183, doi:10.1093/res/hgae007.
bibliographic.title: Late-Medieval Commonplace Culture, the Pastoral Compendium, and the Form of Richard Rolle’s The Form of Living

notes: The complete 38-page accepted manuscript is openly deposited by Cambridge under CC BY 4.0.
Its direct subject is Rolle's Form of Living; its relevance here is the resulting reassessment of one proposed dating argument for Speculum Vitae.

entities.label: British Library Additional MS 33995
entities.description: A late-fourteenth-century English parchment codex. Speculum Vitae occupies ff. 1ra-96rb and is followed by the Stimulus Conscientie Minor, the Prick of Conscience, and a Tractatus/Bande of Louyng.

date_statements.label: manuscript production
date_statements.value: second half of the fourteenth century

holding.notes: No exact item record was recovered from the British Library's current interim Archives and Manuscripts search on 2026-07-14; the shelfmark and holding are independently documented by the thesis and the 1894 British Museum catalogue.

physical_description.support: Parchment; the final leaf is a reused thirteenth-century Latin legal flyleaf
physical_description.dimensions: Leaves average approximately 308 × 200 mm; written frame approximately 245 × 148 mm
notes: The Speculum Vitae scribe writes a late-fourteenth-century anglicana hand, normally about 40-45 lines per column; Robinson localizes the scribal language to northwest Yorkshire.
Latin headings are framed in red, initials have red penwork, and the text includes Latin running titles and marginalia, pointing hands, grotesques, and a terminal Scutum Fidei Trinitatis.
The codex may have belonged to the English Recollects at Douai, later belonged to William Horatio Crawford at Lakelands, Cork, and was bought at the Crawford sale on 18 March 1894.
The physical witness is distinct from Robinson's transcription and from any future photographs.

A whole-manuscript reproduction request should cover bindings, boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every recto and verso through f. 157, edges or color targets where offered, and should separately map Speculum Vitae to ff. 1ra-96rb.

entities.label: Catalogue of Additions to the Manuscripts in the British Museum, 1888-1893
entities.description: The contemporary accession catalogue describing Additional MS 33995 on printed pages 156-157.

bibliographic.citation: British Museum, Catalogue of Additions to the Manuscripts in the British Museum in the Years MDCCCLXXXVIII-MDCCCXCIII (London: British Museum, 1894), 156-157.
bibliographic.title: Catalogue of Additions to the Manuscripts in the British Museum in the Years MDCCCLXXXVIII-MDCCCXCIII

notes: The catalogue inherits the historical attribution to William of Nassyngton; later scholarship rejects that attribution.

entities.label: Digital Index of Middle English Verse record 423
entities.description: The DIMEV v1.0 work record for Speculum Vitae, with IMEV/NIMEV identifier 245, forty-seven witness entries, two ghost references, and an exact entry for Additional MS 33995.
notes: DIMEV v1.0 retains William of Nassington as author in its description. Modern authorship scholarship takes precedence for responsibility metadata.

Its forty-seven witness elements and two ghost references should not be silently equated with Newhauser's statement that the poem is extant in forty-five copies; fragment and counting conventions require reconciliation.

agents.name: Digital Index of Middle English Verse

subject.description: The complete approved repaired-header repository XML

object.description: University of Michigan CME item CME90022

scope.notes: The local artifact preserves the CME identifiers, 2023 header, hierarchy, and encoded text. The exact transfer event into the Kyvero-Press/CME repository is undocumented.
The CME header explicitly identifies raw OTA no. 0022 as the source and describes the added markup.

subject.description: The complete 16,096-line edition in thesis volume 1
The printed machine-readable edition in thesis volume 1

scope.notes: Both are Robinson products titled as machine-readable editions of the same witness and carry the same 16,096-line text, but the exact revision history between the 1983 deposit and 1987 thesis has not been documented.

subject.description: The CME line numbering and hierarchical divisions

scope.notes: The CME header says line numbers and chapter divisions were based on Hanna's edition. It does not say Hanna supplied the base text.

subject.description: DIMEV record 423 witness entry wit-423-26 for BLAdd33995

Open the persistent UMDL name and follow the item interface.
notes: The persistent name resolves to the U-M item resolver; automated access received an HTTP 403 response, so public status is based on the persistent public route and source header rather than a cached page.

Download the OTA resource header XML.
Download both thesis volumes from the Edinburgh Research Archive item.

access.access_method: Consult library holdings or purchase the two-volume EETS set from Oxford University Press.
Download the four-page title-page and table-of-contents preview.

local_copies.label: Hanna edition front matter and tables of contents

work_portion.label: Edition bibliographic and structural preview
locators: title pages and tables of contents only
notes: This is not the edition text and does not include the listed manuscript frontispieces.
Poppler reads and extracts all four pages. qpdf 12.3.2 returns exit 3 with recoverable linearization hint-table/shared-object warnings; the exact provider bytes were retained without repair.

access.access_method: Open the Cambridge repository record or download the complete accepted manuscript directly.

local_copies.label: Glover 2024 complete accepted manuscript
notes: The cached object is the complete 38-page accepted manuscript, not the publisher's version of record.

access.access_method: Apply for a British Library Reader Pass and request the manuscript in the appropriate London reading room, subject to current availability and handling conditions.
No complete public digital facsimile was found in the British Library's current digitized-manuscript resources or exact shelfmark web searches.
notes: Some legacy digital services remain affected by the British Library cyberattack; this status should be rechecked.

access.access_method: Use the British Library digitisation order form, specify shelfmark Add MS 33995, request photography from the original rather than a surrogate, and distinguish reference images from images licensed for reproduction.
access.contact: licensing@bl.uk for licensing and quotation questions; permissions@bl.uk for permission to reuse supplied images
access.format: Commissioned digital photography; exact specifications and delivery depend on quotation
notes: For preservation research, request the complete physical manuscript, not only ff. 1r-96v; map the work's locus separately.

The service warns that manuscript originals are photographed, while scans may come only from existing surrogates; ask explicitly for color photography and whether a legacy surrogate exists before commissioning new work.

access.access_method: Download the complete public-domain catalogue scan from Internet Archive.
Open DIMEV record 423 or download the complete versioned v1.0 data release.

work_portion.label: Speculum Vitae record and represented witness

access.format: HTML and versioned XML data release

rights.component: The underlying fourteenth-century Speculum Vitae text attested by the manuscript, excluding photographs, catalog data, modern transcriptions, editions, and annotations
rights.basis: The anonymous medieval composition and surviving fourteenth-century witness predate modern copyright terms by centuries.
notes: This does not place modern manuscript photographs, transcriptions, editorial markup, or repository delivery files in the public domain.

rights.component: The OTA0022 digital resource as distributed by the Oxford Text Archive
rights.basis: The current OTA item and resource header state a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 3.0 license.
notes: Commercial redistribution is outside this license's NonCommercial grant unless separately authorized.

rights.component: The 2023 U-M/Middle English Compendium encoding and markup represented by the approved CME90022 header
rights.basis: The approved 2023 XML header identifies the resource as available under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 3.0.

rights.component: The checked-out CME90022 XML file, including inherited text and 2023 markup
rights.basis: The file inherits a CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 availability statement from its 2023 header. The CME repository's Unlicense text expressly refers to software and does not clearly relicense inherited transcriptions or editorial data.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 3.0 Unported (inherited header statement)

notes: Treat commercial publication of the XML-derived edition as requiring a separate rights analysis or permission; the public-domain medieval text can be independently transcribed from the witness.

rights.component: Robinson's thesis text, transcription, editorial apparatus, glossary, and studies
rights.basis: The Edinburgh Research Archive provides public downloads but its current item metadata exposes no item-specific open license or rights statement.
notes: Public access is not evidence of permission to republish the thesis PDFs or transcription.

rights.component: Hanna's 2008 reading edition, editorial text, apparatus, introduction, and arrangement
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and other applicable jurisdictions
rights.basis: The modern scholarly edition was published by Oxford University Press in 2008; no open license was found for the complete edition.

rights.component: Any manuscript photographs or other reproductions supplied through a future British Library imaging order
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and contractual terms of supply
rights.basis: British Library imaging terms require permission to copy or reproduce supplied images and direct permission enquiries to permissions@bl.uk; the digitisation service separately directs licensing and quotation questions to licensing@bl.uk. The terms of any future quotation have not yet been received.
notes: Do not place supplied images in a public Git repository or use them in print-on-demand products unless the written terms expressly authorize those uses.

rights.component: The 1894 British Museum catalogue text and the cached Internet Archive scan
rights.basis: The catalogue was published in 1894, and Internet Archive marks the scan with the Public Domain Mark 1.0.

rights.component: DIMEV v1.0 data, documentation, and schemas, including record 423
rights.basis: The DIMEV v1.0 repository LICENSE and README apply Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International to the work.
notes: The license on catalog data does not alter rights in cited editions, manuscript images, or third-party facsimiles.

rights.component: Glover's complete accepted manuscript as deposited by the University of Cambridge
rights.basis: The Cambridge repository metadata explicitly applies Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International to the accepted manuscript.
notes: This license applies to the deposited accepted manuscript and does not change rights in the Review of English Studies version of record or in works discussed by the article.

editorial_practices.representation: Most supplied expansion letters are italicized; frequent nasal abbreviations and several common forms, including with, yat, final e, and, and Latin abbreviations, are silently expanded.
editorial_practices.description: Robinson marks many but not all editorial expansions typographically. Italics in the descendant CME text therefore need source-by-source interpretation and cannot automatically be treated as emphasis or literal manuscript characters.
notes: A manuscript facsimile is required to identify the precise underlying abbreviation sign at each location.

editorial_practices.representation: Uppercase forms are modernized; ambiguous c/f forms are resolved editorially.
editorial_practices.description: The transcription normalizes capitalization and makes stated editorial judgments about ambiguous letterforms.
Robinson explicitly declines to supply punctuation, preserving the absence or presence of punctuation through the transcription conventions rather than modernizing sentence boundaries.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Latin manuscript headings framed in red and red-decorated initials
editorial_practices.representation: Selected manuscript headings are represented as structural HEAD elements; the source color and full decorative context are not encoded as a facsimile.
editorial_practices.description: The 2023 header says manuscript headings were used when convenient. The resulting hierarchy is selective and should not be read as a complete visual record of rubrication.
editorial_practices.scope: Headings and structural divisions across CME90022

editorial_practices.source_feature: The OTA transcription's continuous text and manuscript folio markers
editorial_practices.representation: Line numbers and chapter divisions were aligned with Hanna's 2008 reading edition while the transcription remained based on OTA0022.
editorial_practices.description: The Middle English Compendium consulted Hanna to organize and navigate the inherited transcription; this supporting comparison is not a change of textual base.
editorial_practices.scope: Lineation and DIV hierarchy in the complete encoding

evidence.citation: CME90022 repaired-header XML in the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME submodule, commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.locator: HEADER; IDNO; IDG; VID; BIBNO; EDITORIALDECL; REVISIONDESC; BODY
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the selected repository artifact, identifiers, 2023 revision, OTA source declaration, rights statement, editorial conventions, structural hierarchy, line count, folio markers, and complete encoded text.

evidence.citation: Semantic comparison of the approved repaired-header XML and legacy CME_from_OTA XML at CME commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.locator: legacy SHA-256 15f21b8b0f525a1d5de469df8b286019362f51cfc02310351005173c669a0b0f; git blob 17b68a92ee942686afa5969d20a0b10ae47e6890; approved SHA-256 9794f3ebbd9f1100e463bf1cda79dfcb6b7f9551f52502882bdea14aba01b981
evidence.summary: The legacy file is not strictly well-formed at its wrapper, but recovery parsing found 17,654 nodes in each EEBO subtree and exact equality after whitespace and attribute normalization. The meaningful divergence is the malformed TEMPHEAD wrapper versus the approved full HEADER, not a different medieval text.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library persistent item name CME90022.
evidence.summary: Persistent identifier for the University of Michigan CME item represented by the selected XML. The resolver route existed on 2026-07-14 but blocked the automated client with HTTP 403.

evidence.citation: Oxford Text Archive, Speculum Vitae, resource 0022.
evidence.summary: Official item evidence for the title, handle, public downloads, deposit context, and CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 availability statement. The historical purl.ox.ac.uk identifier is not presented as a current access route because it did not resolve when checked.

evidence.citation: Oxford Text Archive, header0022.xml for Speculum Vitae.
evidence.locator: title, depositor, date deposited, source manuscript, editorial description, availability
evidence.summary: Confirms Christine Robinson as depositor, Edinburgh English as affiliation, deposit on 13 May 1983, British Library Additional MS 33995 as source, and OTA metadata and rights statements.

evidence.citation: Christine Robinson, Speculum Vitae, Oxford Text Archive resource 0022, plain-text transcription.
evidence.summary: The complete direct-source transcription, including line numbering and folio-column markers that identify the work's locus in the manuscript.

evidence.citation: Edinburgh Research Archive item for Christine Robinson's Speculum Vitae thesis, handle 1842/26896.
evidence.summary: Official repository evidence for the complete thesis title, author, PhD status, 1988 repository issue date, and the two publicly downloadable volumes. The current item metadata exposes no item-specific rights or license field.

evidence.citation: Christine Mary Robinson, Speculum Vitae machine-readable edition and thesis, volume 1 (University of Edinburgh PhD thesis, title page 1987).
evidence.locator: title page and abstract; manuscript description; editorial preface; edition pp. 1-424, lines 1-16096
evidence.summary: Primary scholarly evidence for the thesis date, manuscript contents, codicology, script and dialect, decoration and provenance, transcription conventions, complete witness-based edition, and the work's exact folio and line extent.

evidence.citation: Christine Mary Robinson, Speculum Vitae machine-readable edition and thesis, volume 2 (University of Edinburgh PhD thesis, title page 1987).
evidence.locator: glossary pp. 1-259; comparative studies through p. 340; bibliography through p. 350
evidence.summary: Complete second thesis volume, including the glossary and investigation rejecting the claimed common authorship of Speculum Vitae and the Prick of Conscience.

evidence.citation: WorldCat record for Ralph Hanna, Speculum Vitae: A Reading Edition, OCLC 298411327.
evidence.summary: Bibliographic evidence for editor, title, Oxford University Press publication in 2008, EETS Original Series 331-332, two-volume extent, ISBNs, and OCLC identifier.

evidence.citation: Ralph Hanna, Speculum Vitae: A Reading Edition, title pages and tables of contents supplied by ETH Library.
evidence.summary: Partial preview confirming the two volume titles, the credit 'using materials assembled by Venetia Somerset,' and the contents. It contains no edition text and no manuscript frontispiece images despite listing those plates.
notes: Poppler reads and extracts all four pages. qpdf 12.3.2 returns exit 3 with recoverable linearization hint-table/shared-object warnings; the provider file was retained byte-for-byte without repair.

evidence.citation: Richard Newhauser, review of Ralph Hanna, Speculum Vitae: A Reading Edition, Journal of English and Germanic Philology 111, no. 1 (2012): 136–138, doi:10.5406/jenglgermphil.111.1.0136.
evidence.summary: Scholarly synthesis supporting the anonymous authorship, rejection of the William of Nassyngton interpolation, Yorkshire and third-quarter-fourteenth-century context, 16,096 four-stress rhyming-couplet lines, catechetical organization, and selective adaptation of Somme le roi and the Latin Tabula de utilitate oracionis dominice.

evidence.citation: Timothy Glover, ‘Late-Medieval Commonplace Culture, the Pastoral Compendium, and the Form of Richard Rolle’s The Form of Living,’ Review of English Studies 75, no. 319 (2024): 165–183, doi:10.1093/res/hgae007.
evidence.locator: complete 38-page accepted manuscript; discussion of Rolle's sources and implications for Speculum Vitae dating
evidence.summary: The Cambridge record identifies the author, accepted-manuscript status, publication citation and DOI, and CC BY 4.0 license. The article's analysis of The Form of Living supports reconsidering a Rolle-dependent terminus post quem previously used in dating Speculum Vitae, but does not establish a replacement exact date.

evidence.citation: British Museum, Catalogue of Additions to the Manuscripts in the British Museum in the Years MDCCCLXXXVIII-MDCCCXCIII (1894), 156-157.
evidence.summary: Contemporary accession evidence for shelfmark, manuscript contents and folio loci, vellum support, 157 leaves, late-fourteenth-century date, legal flyleaf, Crawford provenance, and the inherited William of Nassyngton attribution.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for CatalogueOfAdditionsToTheMSS188893.
evidence.summary: Confirms the complete PDF filename and the item's Public Domain Mark 1.0 license metadata.

evidence.citation: British Library Digitised Manuscripts and Archives collection page and exact Add MS 33995 public web searches.
evidence.summary: No complete public image object for Add MS 33995 was located through exact shelfmark/title searches of current and archived British Library resources on 2026-07-14. The collection page notes continuing availability limitations after the cyberattack.
notes: This is a dated negative finding and must not be promoted to proof that the manuscript has never been digitized.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation services.
evidence.summary: Current official evidence that individuals and researchers can request reference or reproduction images, that exact shelfmark and folios are required, that original manuscripts receive photography while scans are made only from available surrogates, and that licensing enquiries use licensing@bl.uk.

evidence.citation: British Library, Imaging Services Terms and Conditions, updated January 2026.

evidence.summary: Official provider terms requiring permission to copy or reproduce supplied images and directing permission enquiries to permissions@bl.uk.

evidence.citation: British Library, Reader Pass.
evidence.summary: Current route for obtaining the Reader Pass required to consult collection items in British Library reading rooms.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, version 1.0, record 423, IMEV/NIMEV 245.
evidence.locator: data/Records.xml record-423 and wit-423-26; IMEV/NIMEV 245; manuscript key BLAdd33995; repository LICENSE and README
evidence.summary: Versioned index evidence for the conventional title, normalized incipit, couplet form, spiritual-advice subject, IMEV/NIMEV identifier, forty-seven witness entries, two ghost references, and Additional MS 33995 at ff. 1ra-96rb with matching incipit and explicit. The record's retained William attribution is historical catalog data, not accepted modern authorship. The release LICENSE and README state CC BY 4.0.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME LICENSE at commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: The repository license releases software under the Unlicense but does not clearly state that inherited transcriptions, U-M markup, or provider-supplied content are relicensed; the item header's CC BY-NC-SA statement remains material.

open_questions.question: Does a complete existing digital or photographic surrogate of British Library Additional MS 33995 exist, and can it be supplied with terms permitting research, public Git redistribution, or commercial print-on-demand use?
next_steps: Ask the British Library whether a preservation surrogate, legacy microfilm, or prior color photography already exists before commissioning new work.
If ordering, request every physical surface of the complete codex and map Speculum Vitae to ff. 1ra-96rb rather than ordering only cropped work pages.
Obtain written terms separately for private research, scholarly quotation, public repository redistribution, and commercial or noncommercial print-on-demand publication.

open_questions.question: What revisions, if any, separate Robinson's 13 May 1983 OTA transcription from the edition printed in her 1987 thesis?
next_steps: Computationally collate OTA0022 against the thesis edition after correcting OCR and layout noise.
Seek Robinson's project documentation, deposit correspondence, or thesis production files through the Oxford Text Archive and University of Edinburgh archives.
Keep both products as parallel direct witness transcriptions until an exact revision chain is documented.

open_questions.question: What is the current stable British Library Archives and Manuscripts catalogue URI and present handling status for Add MS 33995?
next_steps: Ask British Library Manuscripts staff for the current item-level record or reference identifier.
Replace the historical-catalog-only holding evidence when an exact current record becomes available.
Recheck the restored catalogue and digitized-content systems after service recovery.

open_questions.question: Do the OTA/U-M CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 terms or any separate permission permit commercial publication of a CME90022-derived edition, and what scope does the CME repository's software-focused Unlicense have over this XML?
next_steps: Request an item-specific clarification from the Oxford Text Archive and University of Michigan Library before commercial reuse.
Distinguish reuse of the public-domain medieval text from reuse of Robinson's transcription, U-M markup, and repository packaging.
For an unrestricted new edition, consider an independent transcription from lawfully obtained manuscript images under written image-use terms.
